scope: Disambiguate the retained 8xMI350 DSv4-style canary, the nearby CE2 A/B peak observation, and the older guarded dense-fallback systems baseline.

target.read: The current pass/fail canary gate is the user-updated 277 TFLOPs/GPU target at MBS=1, S=4096, GBS=128. Token/s and step-time equivalents are derived only for comparability using the retained run's FLOPs/token accounting.

retained_default.read: Canonical retained no-profile anchor because it validates the launcher-selected CE2 default with no chunk override. Under the updated 277 TFLOPs/GPU target, this is a systems/correctness anchor, not a passing performance anchor.

highest_nearby_ce2_ab_observation.read: Nearby manual CE2 A/B was slightly faster than the retained default, but the retained default is the canonical launcher-selected baseline.

old_guarded_dense_fallback_systems_basel.read: This old number is much faster because it is a guarded dense-fallback systems baseline, not the retained sparse/prototype DSv4-style canary. It is still below the updated 277 TFLOPs/GPU target.

flop_accounting_check.read: The TFLOPs/GPU and tok/GPU/s numbers use the same FLOPs/token accounting. The lower retained TFLOPs is explained by lower retained tok/GPU/s, not by a unit conversion mismatch.

read: EP8 reduces local owner-rank MoE VJP from EP4, but dX combine/arrival wait grows and becomes comparable to local VJP.
The highest-return MoE work is a real overlap/skew plan plus a more fused/less padded owner-rank VJP contract, not another blind MORI launch tuple sweep.
Attention projection linears are a mid-sized bucket; the final sparse MLA/LI backward remains necessary, but this guarded canary profile does not exercise the final sparse override.
Chunked CE/lm-head is visible but smaller than the MoE backward path; keep CE2 for this canary while avoiding loss-only tuning as the main ladder.
Profiler kernel-family totals are cumulative GPU event time across streams/ranges and should be used for ranking, not as additive wall time.

bottom_line: Use 3,103 tok/GPU/s, 21.12 s/step, and 58.71 TFLOPs/GPU as the retained 8xMI350 canary anchor. Against the updated 277 TFLOPs/GPU target this is 21.2% of target and needs about 4.72x speedup. The nearby manual CE2 A/B reached about 59.35 TFLOPs/GPU, and the older 204.865 TFLOPs/GPU line belongs to the separate 10,827 tok/GPU/s guarded dense-fallback systems baseline, which is still only 74.0% of the updated target.
